[2023-02-15 09:26] elyot: I stand by our mods. They do a great job. They are complete volunteers and there's not much activity in the game, I'm honestly happy that they're still around taking action on these kinds of things at all. If two players both violated the guidelines, it's usually simpler and fairer to just give the same punishment to both of them rather than try to decide whose violation was more severe.

Good to see you are still playing. 😄
[2023-02-15 09:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): Hi Elyot, I believe that I did not violate the guidelines.
[2023-02-15 09:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): I believe it is okay to give advice to a player as long as the player is fine with it, even if their opponent objects.
[2023-02-15 09:29] elyot: Well the mod did, maybe they messed up, it happens. You're not banned, carry on.
[2023-02-15 09:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): The reason I pinged you is I want to know the rules on giving advice in games in future, because I think the mods interpreted the guidelines wrong.
[2023-02-15 09:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): I want to know exactly what the rules are for the future.
[2023-02-15 09:30] elyot: coaching is OK, kibitzing is not
[2023-02-15 09:30] elyot: I don't think coaching in global chat is a good idea, better to coach over discord or in a call or something
[2023-02-15 09:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): The question is what exactly kibitzing is defined as.
[2023-02-15 09:31] elyot: like if you kibitzed and the person said afterwards "I'm OK with it" then it's still kibitzing
[2023-02-15 09:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): Does this mean that everyone has to ask permission beforehand before commenting on an ongoing game?
[2023-02-15 09:33] elyot: I can't really see any contexts where it would be appropriate to comment without permission
[2023-02-15 09:33] elyot: Like to me, this seems very obvious
[2023-02-15 09:34] silentslayers: I was thinking that not doing it in global chat is the right call
[2023-02-15 09:34] silentslayers: thought the same thing as elyot
[2023-02-15 09:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): It doesn't seem obvious to me - I don't mind being kibitzed, and I believe that the majority of the playerbase doesn't mind being kibitzed.
[2023-02-15 09:35] silentslayers: like if u do it in DMs at least the player not getting advice doesnt realize theyre getting 2v1ed
[2023-02-15 09:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): I think if the majority of the playerbase doesn't mind being kibitzed, an "if asked to stop, stop" policy makes more sense than an "always ask for permission" policy
[2023-02-15 09:35] silentslayers: thats where a sore spot can be
[2023-02-15 09:36] elyot: I think that would be OK for unranked games, but not ladder
[2023-02-15 09:36] elyot: Like if you walked into a chess tournament and just started commenting on a game in progress, that would not be appropriate, and this is very obvious to me
[2023-02-15 09:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): sure, but i think ladder is more like a casual match than a tournament - i obviously don't support kibitz in actual tournaments
[2023-02-15 09:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you look at the motivation on why people play ladder nowadays, id say "playing for fun" is a much higher motivation than "grinding rating points"
[2023-02-15 09:38] silentslayers: I think just comment after the facts is better, like 1 turn later being like "I probably wouldnt have held that splitter" is better than "Hold the splitter!!!" on the exact turn it happens
[2023-02-15 09:39] elyot: "The reason I play the ladder is different from some other people's reason" is not an excuse to violate our guidelines, the guidelines are there to prohibit behaviours that annoy some people, even if they don't annoy you
[2023-02-15 09:41] elyot: In any case, I think I've said enough on the matter, I think everything Wonderboat said makes sense as well. Please try to get along, there are not that many players 😛
[2023-02-15 09:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): Okay, so in summary: Coaching is allowed, kibitzing is not. On a ranked game, permission must be asked first, on an unranked game, it can be done and if told to stop, stop
[2023-02-15 09:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): I can live with that

messages.author: elyot

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: Like to me, this seems very obvious

messages.author: silentslayers

messages.author: masn6811

messages.author: elyot

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: sure, but i think ladder is more like a casual match than a tournament - i obviously don't support kibitz in actual tournaments

messages.author: masn6811

messages.content: "The reason I play the ladder is different from some other people's reason" is not an excuse to violate our guidelines, the guidelines are there to prohibit behaviours that annoy some people, even if they don't annoy you

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: In any case, I think I've said enough on the matter, I think everything Wonderboat said makes sense as well. Please try to get along, there are not that many players 😛

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: Okay, so in summary: Coaching is allowed, kibitzing is not. On a ranked game, permission must be asked first, on an unranked game, it can be done and if told to stop, stop

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: I can live with that

[2023-02-18 02:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Proposed mechanic: p1 and p2 get a 5-second prompt "are you happy with being p1/p2?" (defaults to yes)
* Both yes: game continues as normal
* One of them no: that player gets to bid (see below)
* Both no: p1 gets to bid (see below)

Bidding: (if only p2 voted no above, then swap p1 and p2 in the below outline)
* p1 gets 10-20 seconds with two sliders encoding a bid: one slider enumerates a 1-10 gold amount, the other enumerates a 1-10 turn number

* p1 sets the sliders and then p2 must accept or reject the bid

* If p2 accepts, the players swap sides, and the original p1 must pay their bid on the respective turn

* if p2 rejects, the players maintain their sides, but the original p2 must pay the original p1's bid on the respective turn

* paying a bid involves automatically losing that amount of gold at the start of the respective turn (even going into negative), and the opposing player gaining that gold at the start of their following turn

--
you can turn this mechanic on or off in the settings, like with contributing bonus unit cards. If both players turned it on, it takes effect

[2023-02-18 02:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Potential issue: what if a player stays at negative gold for more than 1 turn? Effectively gaining an "unfair" advantage, at least in terms of somewhat circumventing the bid

Ideas for potential solutions, if it is an issue:
1. At the end of the bid payer's turn, if they are still in the negative, set their gold to 0 and give the opponent the difference at the start of their turn (oppo gets a little less value out of this, depending on "inflation", but hey: it means their opponent didn't spend any gold this turn, so they are probably happy)

2. Reduce the maximum turn number in the slider until there is no longer an issue (assuming that no game exists where one would find a way to gain an advantage out of not trying to pay the bid that early; and assuming there is a maximum turn number < 10 where it is no longer an issue, but >x such that the mechanic is still useful to have)
